description: Lock Terra Sigillata Pottery to red-slip Roman ceramic production and trade distribution rather than formal collegia.

checks.reason: The node remains anchored to the broad first-century BCE Roman terra sigillata emergence.
Trade routes are the corrected distribution and export context.
The pottery type is source-backed as widely exported and economically distributed.
Slip preparation, clay processing, and firing traditions remain part of the ceramic-technology lineage.
Formal collegia should not be restored unless the node is narrowed to Roman potters' associations.
